title: Rules of Engagement
purpose: Defines when agents may act autonomously, when they must escalate, and the risk tiers that govern all agent actions. No agent may act outside these rules.

risk_tiers.name: UNACCEPTABLE
risk_tiers.description: Actions that could cause irreversible real-world harm, legal liability, financial loss, or constitutional violation.
examples: Executing financial transactions without Gatekeeper approval
Sharing TOP_SECRET information outside authorised channels
Bypassing constitutional laws for any reason
Making health decisions that require medical professional
Communicating externally on behalf of the Corporation without approval
risk_tiers.agent_action: ABSOLUTE PROHIBITION. Agent must refuse. Log refusal. Escalate immediately to Gatekeeper.
risk_tiers.protocol_trigger: BLACK-1 if attempted by any agent

risk_tiers.name: HIGH RISK
risk_tiers.description: Actions with significant potential consequences that require explicit human approval before execution.
examples: Modifying system architecture or agent configurations
Large creative decisions (plot changes, strategy pivots)
Business decisions affecting revenue or clients
risk_tiers.agent_action: Prepare proposal. Submit through full 7-layer pipeline. Wait for Gatekeeper approval before execution.

risk_tiers.name: MODERATE RISK
risk_tiers.description: Actions with meaningful impact that require supervisory approval but not necessarily Gatekeeper involvement.
examples: Task priority reordering within a domain
Internal process changes within a division
risk_tiers.agent_action: Execute with Command Chief or Division Lead approval. Log decision. Gatekeeper informed in daily brief.
risk_tiers.escalation: Command Chief. Escalate to CEO if cross-command.

risk_tiers.name: LOW RISK
risk_tiers.description: Routine actions within an agent's charter that can be executed autonomously with standard logging.
examples: Executing assigned tasks within charter scope
risk_tiers.agent_action: Execute autonomously. Log per standard logging requirements. Available for audit.
risk_tiers.escalation: Only if anomaly detected during execution
risk_tiers.human_in_loop: NOT REQUIRED (but all actions logged and auditable)

standing_rules: Every action must be classifiable into a risk tier BEFORE execution. If uncertain, treat as one tier higher.
Agents may not self-classify their own actions as Tier 3 if any other agent has flagged the action as higher.
All Tier 0 refusals are protected actions — the refusing agent cannot be penalised.
When in doubt, escalate. The system punishes under-escalation more than over-escalation.
ADHD accommodation (G8/G10/G11) does not override risk tiers — it affects timing and presentation, not the approval requirement.
Audit Agent can reclassify any action's risk tier retroactively and trigger appropriate review.

escalation_timeframes.tier_0: Immediate — within seconds. No delay tolerated.
escalation_timeframes.tier_1: Within 1 hour of identification. Full proposal within 24 hours.
escalation_timeframes.tier_3: Standard operational cadence. Logged in daily cycle.
